title: Use and Make Line Plots
essential_question: How can you read and make line plots to show data?
strategies: Each X above a number means one data point
Count the Xs to find how many
Line plots often show measurement data

pretest.question: A line plot shows shirt prices. There are 4 Xs above $12. How many shirts sold for $12?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: On a line plot, what does each X represent?
pretest.explanation: Each X on a line plot represents one data point.

learn.title: What is a Line Plot?
learn.content: A line plot uses Xs (or dots) above a number line to show data. Each X represents one piece of data. The number line shows the values (like measurements or prices).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Reading a Line Plot
learn.content: To find how many for a value, count the Xs above that number. To find the total, count ALL the Xs. To find 'most,' look for the tallest stack of Xs.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Making a Line Plot
learn.content: Step 1: Draw a number line with the values from your data. Step 2: Write a title. Step 3: For each data point, draw an X above the correct number. Step 4: Check that your total Xs match the total data points.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 라인 플롯 만들기 실전 연습
learn.content: 데이터: 연필 길이(인치) — 6, 6½, 6, 7, 6½, 6, 7½, 6½. 단계 1: 최솟값(6) ~ 최댓값(7½) 수직선 그리기. 단계 2: 제목 쓰기 — '연필 길이'. 단계 3: 각 데이터 위에 X 표시: 6인치=X X X / 6½인치=X X X / 7인치=X X / 7½인치=X. 단계 4: X 개수(8) = 데이터 수(8) 확인 ✓. 실수 주의: ½ 눈금을 통째로 건너뛰지 않는다.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: '미만'과 '이하' 구별하기
learn.content: 미만(less than / <) → 경계값 포함 안 함. 이하(less than or equal to / ≤) → 경계값 포함. 예: '3인치 미만' → 1인치, 2인치만 세기 (3인치 제외). 예: '3인치 이하' → 1인치, 2인치, 3인치 세기. 팁: '미만'=열린 동그라미, '이하'=닫힌 동그라미로 표시.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 최빈값(가장 많은 값) 찾기
learn.content: 챔피언 스캔: 왼쪽 → 오른쪽으로 X 개수 비교. 1단계: 첫 번째 열을 현재 챔피언으로 설정. 2단계: 오른쪽으로 이동하며 더 높은 스택 찾기. 3단계: 더 높으면 새 챔피언 교체. 4단계: 끝까지 스캔 → 챔피언 = 최빈값. 동점이면 두 값 모두 최빈값으로 보고.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Math Talk: '이상'과 '초과' 구별하기
learn.content: 이상(at least / ≥) → 경계값 포함. 초과(more than / >) → 경계값 포함 안 함. 예: '5인치 이상' → 5, 5½, 6 모두 세기. 예: '5인치 초과' → 5½, 6만 세기 (5 제외). 완전 정리: 미만(<) 제외 | 이하(≤) 포함 | 초과(>) 제외 | 이상(≥) 포함.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 라인 플롯 4가지 핵심 전략 요약
learn.content: 전략 1 특정값 개수: 그 값 위 X만 세기 — 레이블을 답으로 쓰지 말 것. 전략 2 전체 합계: 모든 X를 빠짐없이 세기 — 한 열만 세지 말 것. 전략 3 범위 비교: 경계 포함/제외 확인 후 해당 열만 합산. 전략 4 최빈값: 챔피언 스캔으로 가장 높은 스택 찾기. 추가: ½·¼ 눈금은 반드시 수직선에 표시한다.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Line plot of ribbon lengths: 4 in=1X, 4½ in=3X, 5 in=5X, 5½ in=2X, 6 in=1X. How many ribbons are shorter than 5½ inches?
hints: 'Shorter than 5½' means strictly less than — do NOT include 5½.
Add only the values at 4, 4½, and 5.

feedback.correct: Excellent! You correctly excluded the boundary value.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember: 'shorter than 5½' excludes 5½ itself.

solution_steps: Identify: less than 5½ means < 5½ (do not include 5½).
try.feedback_correct: Excellent! You correctly excluded the boundary value.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Caterpillar length line plot: 1 in=2X, 1½ in=3X, 2 in=6X, 2½ in=4X, 3 in=1X. What is the difference between the count of the most common length and the least common length?
try.explanation: Most common: 2 in (6 Xs). Least common: 3 in (1 X). 6−1=5.
hints: Find the tallest stack (most) and the shortest stack (least).
Subtract: most count − least count.

feedback.correct: Great! You found both the mode and minimum count correctly.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Find the tallest stack (6) and shortest (1), then subtract.

solution_steps: Champion scan: 2 in has 6 Xs — most common.
Smallest stack: 3 in has 1 X — least common.
try.feedback_correct: Great! You found both the mode and minimum count correctly.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r2.question: Line plot: 1 in=2X, 1½ in=3X, 2 in=6X, 2½ in=4X, 3 in=1X. If you add 3 more items that are each 1½ inches, what would be the new most common length?
choices: A. 1½ inches (6 Xs) — tied with 2 inches
B. 2 inches (6 Xs) — still the most
C. 1½ inches (6 Xs) — the new most
practice_r2.explanation: 1½ becomes 3+3=6. 2 inches is also 6. They're tied.
hints: Think about what use and make line plots means.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is A. Remember how use and make line plots works.

practice_r2.question: Worm lengths: 3 in=1X, 3¼ in=2X, 3½ in=4X, 3¾ in=3X, 4 in=2X. If you add 2 more worms that are each 3¾ inches, what is the new most common length?
practice_r2.explanation: 3¾ goes from 3→5 Xs. Now 5 > 4 (3½). New mode: 3¾ inches.
hints: Update 3¾: it had 3 Xs, add 2 more → now 5 Xs.
Compare updated stacks: 3½=4, 3¾=5. Which is taller?

feedback.correct: Great reasoning! You recalculated the mode after updating.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. 3¾ becomes 5 Xs, which beats 3½ (4 Xs).

practice_r2.feedback_correct: Great reasoning! You recalculated the mode after updating.

practice_r2.question: A bar graph shows fruit counts: Apples=15, Oranges=9, Bananas=12. How many more apples are there than oranges?
hints: Find Apples (15) and Oranges (9).
Subtract: bigger − smaller.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. 15−9=6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.2 Lesson 2.7 'Use and Make Line Plots' pp.89-92
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 6 Lessons 3-4 — Collecting and Displaying Data (Line Plots)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Line plot of heights in inches: 48=1X, 49=3X, 50=5X, 51=4X, 52=2X. The average height is closest to which number?
hints: Think about what use and make line plots means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how use and make line plots works.

practice_r3.question: 262 hamburgers rounded to the nearest hundred is?
practice_r3.explanation: 262: tens digit is 6 ≥ 5 → round up to 300.
hints: Think about what use and make line plots means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is D. Remember how use and make line plots works.

practice_r3.question: Line plot: 5 in=2X, 5½ in=4X, 6 in=6X, 6½ in=3X, 7 in=1X. How many items are between 5½ and 6½ inches (inclusive)?
hints: Think about what use and make line plots means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how use and make line plots works.
